2606.27237.title: LMs as Task-Specific Knowledge Bases: An Interpretability Analysis
2606.27237.paper_summary: Language models (LMs) capture large amounts of factual knowledge applicable to a wide range of tasks, motivating the view of their parameters as a knowledge base. An important property of knowledge bases is that different queries for the same fact return consistent results, drawing on a single source of truth. We investigate whether LMs satisfy this property through behavioral and mechanistic analyses. Our results suggest that they encode knowledge in a task-specific manner. Behaviorally, facts acquired on one task frequently fail to co-emerge on others during training. Parameter localization experiments suggest a mechanistic explanation, revealing distinct parameter subsets underlying different tasks for the same fact. Finally, we show that chain-of-thought reasoning draws part of its effectiveness from engaging task-specific parameters beyond those tied to the evaluation task. Our findings suggest that what the model knows and how it is asked are intertwined in parameter space, undermining the "knowledge base" analogy and carrying implications for the reliability and controllability of factual knowledge in LMs.
2606.27237.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型（LMs）捕捉大量適用於廣泛任務的事實知識，這使得將其參數視為知識庫的觀點得以成立。知識庫的一個重要特性是，對於同一事實的不同查詢會返回一致的結果，依賴於單一的真實來源。我們通過行為和機理分析來研究 LMs 是否滿足這一特性。我們的結果表明，它們以任務特定的方式編碼知識。在行為上，在一個任務上獲得的事實在訓練期間經常無法在其他任務上共同出現。參數定位實驗提供了一種機理解釋，揭示了對於同一事實，不同任務所依賴的不同參數子集。最後，我們展示了思維鏈推理的有效性部分來自於參與任務特定參數的運用，這些參數超出了與評估任務相關的參數。我們的發現表明，模型所知的內容和提問的方式在參數空間中是交織在一起的，這削弱了“知識庫”類比的有效性，並對 LMs 中事實知識的可靠性和可控性帶來影響。
2606.27237.author: Amit Elhelo et.al.
2606.27237.authors: Amit Elhelo, Amir Globerson, Mor Geva
